published_prior_art.cite: ScharfsteinManskiAnthony2004: bounded missing-outcome distributions using constrained conditional log-odds selection-bias parameters; binary no-baseline case gives the same armwise odds-ratio completion.

ScharfsteinManskiAnthony2004: treatment-effect bounds under intervention-specific selection-bias log-odds constraints; the proposed common-error coupling is not clearly covered but is currently ill-posed.

repo_prior_art.cite: CausalSmith/doc/API.md 3.1 ManskiNonparametricBounds_Study: bounded-outcome Manski intervals are the limiting comparator.

published_prior_art.cite: ScharfsteinManskiAnthony2004: finite bounds on missing-outcome distributions under bounded selection-bias parameters imply strict tightening relative to worst-case bounds in the nondegenerate binary case.

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The armwise binary odds-ratio missing-outcome bound is already contained in Scharfstein, Manski, and Anthony (2004)'s class-based bounds with bounded conditional log-odds selection-bias parameters.

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 2
novelty_flags.one_line: Strict finite-parameter tightening over worst-case missing-outcome bounds is a direct consequence of the same ScharfsteinManskiAnthony2004 class-based selection-bias bounds, not a new published-axis theorem.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The literature review misses ScharfsteinManskiAnthony2004, which is closer than Greenland/VanderWeele for sharp randomized-trial missing-outcome bounds under interpretable selection-bias parameters.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1 / tier floor
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=field below novelty_target=flagship; to reach flagship the proposal must prove a genuinely new open-class nonrectangular compatibility frontier beyond ScharfsteinManskiAnthony2004, not just restate rectangular armwise bounds.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: Assumption common-error uses one binary V whose (E_d,V) margins equal both arm-specific C(d) tables, which forces P(V=1)=s_0=s_1 unless an additional treatment-specific selection map is defined.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The [0,1]-valued Y extension is not well-defined: the displayed odds ratio and 2x2 table are binary-outcome objects, and the layer-cake sentence does not define a threshold-wise or extremal-law restriction.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add Scharfstein, Manski, and Anthony (2004, Biometrics) as the primary comparator and explicitly derive the binary no-baseline reduction before stating the remaining epsilon-gap.
Replace the single common V with either treatment-specific V_0,V_1 or a shared latent U plus treatment-specific threshold maps so different observed selection rates s_0 and s_1 are allowed.
Restrict the main theorem to binary Y, or define a threshold-indexed odds-ratio budget for bounded Y and prove the layer-cake projection preserves the same endpoints.

For flagship viability, prove an open-set separation between the rectangular Scharfstein-style armwise interval and the true common-structural-error sharp set, with computable compatibility inequalities.

recommended_next_step: Revise around ScharfsteinManskiAnthony2004 and fix the common-error/selection-rate compatibility before resubmitting.
